meta_title: Impact Doors in Emerald Hills, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Emerald Hills, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, $0 down financing. Protect your home—call today!
h1: Impact Doors for Emerald Hills Homes: Stronger Protection, Lasting Peace of Mind
intro:
Emerald Hills is one of Broward County's most desirable residential communities — a well-established neighborhood where mature tree-lined streets, top-rated schools, and proximity to the Emerald Hills Country Club have helped sustain average home values near $550,000. For homeowners who have invested significantly in this community, protecting that investment starts at the front door — literally. Impact doors aren't just a smart upgrade here; given Broward County's wind zone requirements and the area's documented storm history, they are increasingly a code-mandated necessity for renovation and replacement projects alike.

Situated approximately five miles from the Atlantic coastline, Emerald Hills sits in a wind zone rated for 170 mph wind speeds. While the area falls just outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), Florida Building Code still mandates that all exterior door replacements in this region meet hurricane-rated standards. That means standard fiberglass or hollow-core steel doors simply won't pass inspection — and more importantly, they won't hold up when a serious storm makes landfall near Hollywood, Davie, or Cooper City. The pressure differentials created by strong hurricanes are more than capable of breaching a non-rated door, exposing your entire home to catastrophic wind and rain intrusion.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we specialize in helping Emerald Hills homeowners select and install the right impact doors for their specific home layout, architectural style, and performance needs. Whether you're upgrading a grand entry door, a set of French doors overlooking the backyard, or wide sliding glass doors leading to a patio, we carry a full line of ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact door systems designed to meet or exceed all applicable Florida Building Code requirements. With over 500 homes completed across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, our team brings unmatched local expertise to every project — and our 60+ years of combined installation experience means your new doors will be done right the first time.

section_why.h2: Why Emerald Hills Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Replacement Projects
section_why.body:
When you call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not calling a general contractor who subcontracts your work to the lowest bidder. Every installation we perform is handled entirely by our own in-house crews — trained, licensed, and experienced professionals who have installed impact doors in hundreds of South Florida homes. For Emerald Hills residents, this distinction matters enormously. Our installers understand the specific construction characteristics of the neighborhood's housing stock, including the 40% of local homes built before 1994 that often feature non-standard rough openings, older framing conditions, or original door frames that require careful attention during replacement.

Our process begins with a no-pressure, in-home consultation where we assess your existing doors, measure every opening with precision, and walk you through product options that align with your aesthetic preferences and your budget. We handle the full scope of work: product selection, permitting through the City of Hollywood Building Division, professional installation, and final inspection coordination. You won't be left chasing down a permit or scheduling a third-party crew — we manage it all under one roof, which is why homeowners throughout Emerald Hills, Hollywood, and the surrounding communities consistently give us A+ ratings with the Better Business Bureau.

Our 60+ years of combined team experience also means we've seen every curveball a South Florida home can throw — from hurricane damage repairs to complex multi-door installations in high-end properties. Impact doors require precision fitting to perform correctly under extreme wind loads; even a small gap in the frame or improper sealing can compromise the door's rated performance in a storm. Our crews are trained specifically in the installation protocols required by ESW and CWI product certifications, ensuring that your doors not only look beautiful but perform exactly as engineered when a storm threatens the Emerald Hills community.

Finally, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options, making it practical for homeowners at every budget level to get the protection their home deserves. We believe that living in a community as established and valued as Emerald Hills shouldn't mean choosing between quality and affordability — so we've built a financing program that makes both achievable.

section_history.h2: A Storm-Tested Community: Emerald Hills' Hurricane History and What It Means for Your Doors
section_history.body:
Emerald Hills and the broader Hollywood area have experienced nine named storms since 1990, giving residents a firsthand education in what South Florida weather is truly capable of. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a turning point for the entire region — a Category 5 storm that devastated communities across Broward and Miami-Dade counties and exposed just how vulnerable homes with standard doors and windows were to catastrophic wind pressure. Andrew was the catalyst for major revisions to Florida's building codes, and the damage it caused in communities like Emerald Hills underscored that even homes several miles from the ocean face serious structural risk during a major hurricane.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought a different kind of threat — rapid intensification, broad wind fields, and debris-laden gusts that damaged roofs, shattered unprotected glass doors, and sent projectile debris through poorly sealed entryways across Broward County. Wilma reinforced what Andrew had already shown: that entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors are among the most vulnerable points of a home's envelope during a hurricane. And then Hurricane Irma arrived in 2017, tracking up the Florida peninsula and producing sustained tropical-storm-force winds across the Hollywood and Emerald Hills area for hours on end — enough to exploit any weakness in an aging door system.

For the approximately 40% of Emerald Hills homes built before 1994 — before updated post-Andrew building codes took effect — original door systems may be decades past their useful service life and were never engineered to meet today's wind-resistance standards. Replacing those doors with properly rated impact systems isn't just about preparing for the next storm; it's about correcting a structural vulnerability that has existed in these homes for 30 or more years. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team understands this reality and approaches every Emerald Hills project with the urgency and care that South Florida storm history demands.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Emerald Hills and Broward County
section_code.body:
Emerald Hills falls within Broward County's wind-borne debris region, and while the area sits just outside the boundaries of the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — which applies to Miami-Dade and the eastern portions of Broward — the Florida Building Code still mandates that all new and replacement exterior doors meet hurricane-rated performance standards. Specifically, any door installed in this wind zone must be rated to withstand design pressures consistent with 170 mph wind speeds, and all products must be Florida Product Approved with valid Notice of Acceptance (NOA) documentation. Non-rated doors cannot legally be installed as replacements in this area and will not pass final inspection.

All impact door installations in Emerald Hills require permits pulled through the City of Hollywood Building Division, which oversees permitting for properties within Hollywood's jurisdiction. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on behalf of our clients — from submitting product specifications and installation drawings to scheduling and coordinating the final inspection. This is a critical step that some homeowners overlook when hiring unlicensed or out-of-area contractors. An unpermitted door installation can create significant problems when you attempt to sell your home, file an insurance claim, or make additional improvements — issues that Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's clients simply never have to worry about.

Product approval documentation for doors in Broward County's non-HVHZ wind-borne debris region must demonstrate compliance with ASTM E1886 and E1996 large-missile impact testing protocols, as well as cycling pressure tests that simulate the repeated stress a door faces over the duration of a hurricane. The ESW and CWI impact door systems we install carry the product approvals and impact ratings required to satisfy Broward County inspectors and meet Florida Building Code Chapter 16 requirements. Our team stays current on code updates so that every installation we complete is fully compliant from the first permit application to the final inspector sign-off.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection Built for Emerald Hills Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most respected impact door product lines in South Florida: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both manufacturers produce impact-rated entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French door systems specifically engineered for the demands of Florida's coastal climate — combining heavy-duty aluminum or reinforced fiberglass frames with laminated impact glass and multi-point locking systems that secure the door at multiple points along the frame rather than at a single central latch. This multi-point locking technology dramatically increases the door's resistance to wind pressure and forced entry, making it a meaningful upgrade in both storm protection and everyday home security.

ESW impact doors are particularly well-suited for Emerald Hills homes where aesthetic flexibility matters. Their product lineup includes a range of decorative glass options, frame finishes, and sidelite configurations that allow homeowners to maintain or enhance their home's curb appeal while achieving full hurricane-rated performance. For homes near the Emerald Hills Country Club corridor where architectural character and street presence contribute to property values, ESW's design options make it possible to upgrade without compromising visual appeal. CWI products offer comparable performance with particular strength in large-format sliding glass door systems — ideal for Emerald Hills homes with rear patios, pool decks, or open-plan living spaces that rely on wide glass door openings.

Both ESW and CWI products carry Florida Product Approval and are certified to meet or exceed the impact and pressure ratings required for Broward County's 170 mph wind zone. Their laminated glass cores — typically featuring a PVB or SGP interlayer bonded between two panes of tempered glass — are designed to remain intact even when struck by hurricane debris, preventing the breach that allows catastrophic wind and water intrusion into your home. For Emerald Hills homeowners, these products represent the ideal combination of engineering rigor, design quality, and long-term durability in one of the most demanding climates in North America.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Doors Cost in Emerald Hills? Investment, Financing, and Long-Term Value
section_cost.body:
For a home valued near the Emerald Hills average of $550,000, investing in impact doors is both a meaningful protection upgrade and a measurable enhancement to property value. The actual cost of impact door installation varies based on the number of doors being replaced, the size and style of each unit, and the complexity of the existing framing conditions. A single impact-rated entry door installation in Emerald Hills typically ranges from $2,500 to $5,500 installed, while sliding glass door systems — particularly wider multi-panel configurations — can range from $4,000 to $10,000 or more depending on size. Whole-home door replacement projects spanning entry doors, French doors, and multiple sliders are often packaged together for better overall value.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options, making it straightforward for Emerald Hills homeowners to move forward with their project without depleting savings or waiting until conditions worsen. Our financing is designed to be flexible and accessible — a particularly important feature given that many homeowners in this community are balancing mortgage obligations on higher-value properties with ongoing home improvement priorities. The ability to spread the cost of impact door replacement over time while beginning to realize insurance savings and storm protection benefits immediately makes financing a genuinely smart financial strategy.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact doors in Broward County frequently deliver meaningful value in two ways: first, through reductions in homeowners' insurance premiums following wind mitigation verification (often ranging from 10% to 30% on the wind portion of your policy); and second, through demonstrable increases in home resale value. In a competitive market like Emerald Hills — where buyers are savvy, home values are substantial, and storm preparedness is a top priority — fully impact-rated doors are a selling point that directly influences buyer confidence and offer price. The upgrade essentially pays for itself over time while protecting everything else you've built.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Emerald Hills Homeowners Who Upgrade to Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial reasons to install impact doors in Emerald Hills is the potential for significant homeowners' insurance savings. Florida's insurance market — which has become increasingly difficult for Broward County homeowners in recent years, with carriers tightening underwriting standards and raising premiums — actively rewards homes that demonstrate verified hurricane mitigation improvements. Impact doors are among the qualifying upgrades that can trigger meaningful premium reductions, and in many cases the annual savings help offset the cost of the installation itself over a relatively short timeframe.

The mechanism for capturing these savings is the wind mitigation inspection, a formal process conducted by a licensed inspector who documents the hurricane-resistant features of your home. When your impact doors meet Florida Building Code impact and pressure ratings — which all ESW and CWI products installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing do — those features are documented on a wind mitigation report submitted to your insurance carrier. Major carriers operating in Broward County, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance, use this documentation to calculate opening protection credits that can reduce the wind portion of your premium by 10% to 30% or more depending on your policy structure and current coverage.

For a homeowner in Emerald Hills carrying a policy on a $550,000 property, a 15% to 25% reduction in the wind portion of their premium can translate to hundreds of dollars in annual savings — year after year for the life of the doors. Over a 10- to 15-year horizon, those cumulative savings represent a substantial offset against the initial cost of installation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing encourages all Emerald Hills clients to schedule a wind mitigation inspection after their impact door installation is complete and inspected, and we're happy to provide the product certification documentation your inspector will need to accurately record your home's upgraded protection status.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors if Emerald Hills isn't in the HVHZ?
faqs.a: Yes. While Emerald Hills falls just outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone, Broward County's wind-borne debris region designation still requires that all replacement exterior doors meet hurricane-rated performance standards under Florida Building Code. With the area rated for 170 mph wind speeds and a documented history of hurricane impacts including Andrew, Wilma, and Irma, impact doors are both a legal requirement for permitted replacements and a critical protection upgrade for your home.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Emerald Hills, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Emerald Hills typically range from $2,500 to $5,500 for a single entry door installation and $4,000 to $10,000+ for sliding glass or French door systems, depending on size and configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options, making it easy to move forward with your project right away while spreading the investment over time.

faqs.q: Who handles the permit for impact door installation in Emerald Hills?
faqs.a: Impact door installations in Emerald Hills require permits through the City of Hollywood Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf — including submitting product specifications, installation drawings, and Florida Product Approval documentation — and coordinates the final inspection. You won't need to deal with the permitting office directly; we handle it all as part of our full-service installation process.

faqs.q: How long does it take to get impact doors installed in Emerald Hills?
faqs.a: From initial consultation to completed installation, most Emerald Hills impact door projects take approximately four to eight weeks, depending on product lead times and permit processing at the City of Hollywood Building Division. The actual installation itself is typically completed in one to two days for most residential projects. We'll provide a clear project timeline during your consultation so you know exactly what to expect at each stage of the process.

faqs.q: Will impact doors actually lower my homeowners' insurance premium in Broward County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. After your impact doors are installed and inspected, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your home's opening protection upgrades on a formal report submitted to your insurance carrier. Major carriers operating in Broward County use this documentation to apply opening protection credits that can reduce the wind portion of your premium by 10% to 30% or more annually. For Emerald Hills homes insured at or near the $550,000 average value, these savings can be substantial and recur year after year.

cta_body: Don't wait for the next storm to find out whether your doors are truly up to the challenge. Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a no-pressure, in-home consultation and discover why Emerald Hills homeowners call us the Kings of Service.
